title: echium angustifolium subsp. sericeum
intro: Echium angustifolium subsp. sericeum, also known as the Silver-leaved Viper's Bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Echium angustifolium subsp. sericeum include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with colds and flu, as well as its potential to help alleviate allergies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
